public_links.title: Appropedia: Open-Source Passive Cooling & Ventilation Designs
ScienceDirect: Aerodynamic and Thermal Performance Analysis of Windcatcher Systems

technical_documents.description: An off-grid passive HVAC infrastructure blueprint inspired by traditional Persian Badgir engineering. By utilizing a multi-directional structural tower core paired with sub-surface capillary evaporative media, this schema exploits natural wind pressure differentials to capture, cool, and circulate fresh atmospheric streams into living spaces with zero utility dependency.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Rapidly urbanizing hot-climate environments suffer from extreme heat island effects, forcing households to deploy high-wattage (1,500W+) mechanical AC units. This continuous cooling demand overloads regional energy grids, drives skyrocketing carbon emissions, and financially excludes over 60% of low-income families from safe indoor thermal comfort.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The design features a 2.5m vertical square-section tower framework housing four isolated wind-scoop quadrants configured to capture omni-directional prevailing boundary layer winds. As air stream energy impacts the windward quadrant, a high-pressure zone is generated, forcing air downward. The moving mass crosses a gravity-saturated porous cellulose matrix where fluid latent heat conversion occurs via rapid micro-droplet vaporization. This thermodynamic drop drives cooled, dense air into internal living zones through a floor-level low-velocity diffuser, fueled entirely by fluid buoyancy and kinetic wind shear forces without mechanical fans.

bill_of_materials.name: Corrosion-Resistant Galvanised Steel Sheets (1m x 2m, x4)
Rigid PVC Downpipe Duct & Heavy-Duty Floor Vent Grill
Stainless Steel Condensed Fluid Drip Tray & Overflow Pipe Assembly

specifications.cooling_mechanism: Passive evaporative fluid-dynamics via directional wind pressure differential

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Construct a 2.5m tall square tower on the windward side of the building; mount on roof or adjacent wall.
Step 2: Fabricate four directional scoops from galvanised sheet; weld to tower top facing N/S/E/W.
Step 3: Install cellulose pad horizontally mid-tower; connect drip tray to a small header tank.
Step 4: Run PVC duct from tower base through wall to floor-level vent inside the room.
Step 5: Add insect mesh to all openings; test with incense smoke to verify downward airflow path.

comments.text: Dropped our living room from 34°C to 28°C during peak summer. Zero electricity.
